world.title: Lebanon ceasefire extended by three weeks
world.description: The ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel has been extended for three weeks after a high-level meeting at the White House, US President Donald Trump has said.

world.title: 'America First' aid policy reshapes how U.S. delivers global health assistance
world.description: Since the dismantling of USAID, the Trump administration has been revamping aid policies, focusing on smaller deals with recipient governments. Countries receiving American aid will be required to finance part, and eventually, the entire program. Fred de Sam Lazaro reports from Kenya and Uganda, two nations that have signed agreements under the new America First Global Health Strategy.

world.title: Lebanon and Israel agree to extend ceasefire for another 3 weeks, Trump says
world.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks after talks at the White House on Thursday, according to U.S. President Donald Trump.

world.title: Trump Says 'The Clock is Ticking'
world.description: Amid rising US-Iran tensions, Trump warns the 'clock is ticking' for Tehran as a third US aircraft carrier arrives in the Middle East.

world.title: Ever wanted to grow a giant pumpkin?
world.description: If so, this is your chance to learn how at an upcoming library event

world.title: Pope Leo vents about failure to end Iran war: 'Many innocent people have died'
world.description: ABOARD THE PAPAL PLANE — Pope Leo expressed frustration Thursday that U.S. and Iranian leaders have not been able to get the diplomatic efforts to end the war back on track.“One day Iran says 'yes,' the United States says 'no,' and vice versa,” Leo told reporters on the flight back to the Vatican after an 11-day pastoral visit to Africa.

world.title: Trump orders Navy to 'shoot and kill'; President believes King Charles can help ease tensions; Peace talks continue
world.description: T﻿rump has ordered the Navy to fire "without hesitation" if approached.

business.title: MrBeast's production company sued over alleged sexual harassment
business.description: A former Beast Industries employee is suing MrBeast's media production company after she was allegedly fired from her social media manager job upon returning from maternity leave and following years of what she described as sexual harassment and workplace gender bias.

business.title: China stockpiled three times as much crude as the US in 2025
business.description: The US government report underscores how intensely China worked to bolster its reserves ahead of the Iran war.

business.title: Shares jittery, oil advances on US
business.description:
Global shares swung between gains and losses on Friday and oil prices resumed their rise, as a shaky ceasefire in the Middle East war and stalled US-Iran peace talks gave investors little to cheer and dampened the market mood.
Futures pointed to a dour opening in Europe, as EUROSTOXX 50 futures STXEc1 slid 0.7% and FTSE futures FFIc1 lost 0.76%, while DAX futures FDXc1 eased 0.25%.

business.title: The AI effect: Meta slashes 8000 jobs, Microsoft set for redundancies
business.description: Meta is laying off about 10 per cent of its workforce, the company said as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.

business.title: With the Iran War In a Stalemate, Software Takes Center Stage
business.description: President Trump seems willing to let the ceasefire sit, while control of the Strait of Hormuz hangs in the balance.

science.title: Canadian astronaut Joshua Kutryk joins NASA's SpaceX mission
science.description: OTTAWA — Canadian astronaut Joshua Kutryk is set to head to the International Space Station later this year, joining NASA’s SpaceX Crew-13. A news release from the Canadian Space Agency said it will be Kutryk’s first space mission. He will be the eighth Canadian Space Agency astronaut to fly to the International Space Station and […]

science.title: Canadian astronaut Joshua Kutryk announced as part of a crew heading to the International Space Station
science.description: Hot off the heels of the Artemis II mission around the moon that included Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, another Canadian has been announced for another space mission.

science.title: Canadian Space Agency astronaut Joshua Kutryk assigned to Crew
science.description: Today, the Honourable Mélanie Joly, Minister of Industry and Minister responsible for Canada Economic Development for Quebec Regions, announced that Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Colonel Joshua Kutryk has been assigned to NASA’s SpaceX Crew-13, an International Space Station mission.

science.title: U.S. Applauds Jordan's Signing of Artemis Accords
science.description: The Department of State congratulates the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan on joining the Artemis Accords. Jordan is the 63rd country to sign the Accords

ai.title: Meta to lay off 10% of workforce in AI push
ai.description: Meta plans to lay off 10% of its employees worldwide -- about 8,000 people -- and focus on artificial intelligence, an employee memo said Thursday.

ai.title: Meta to lay off 8,000 employees in AI investment pivot
ai.description: Meta confirms plans to cut 10% of its workforce — roughly 8,000 jobs — starting May to prioritize heavy investments in artificial intelligence.

ai.title: OpenAI unveils GPT-5.5 to field tasks with limited instructions
ai.description: The ChatGPT maker on Thursday unveiled GPT 5.5, a new model that it says is better at aiding scientists, streamlining software development and carrying out more complex tasks.

ai.title: Trump administration targets foreign exploitation of US AI models
ai.description: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies’ exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.
